100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On April 17, 1926, the U.S. Senate began debate on the controversial McNary-Haugen Bill, aimed at supporting struggling farmers through government price supports. Across the Atlantic, Britain faced rising tensions with Egypt as nationalists intensified their demands for independence. Meanwhile, in France, a new coalition government led by Prime Minister Edgard Clémentel was formed amidst ongoing economic challenges.
